commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human maintainer review is needed because the remaining blockers are prerequisite merge/rebase, product/security API acceptance, dependency approval, and contributor proof rather than one narrow automated repair.; Needs attention: The diff introduces security-sensitive browser-control/node-event surfaces and dependency graph changes that need maintainer/security review before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Writ... (sha=debaa45e3e2aea370b91aa8a300276aeb73c3484)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a bundled Chrome extension/My Browser driver and node-owned CDP bridge, routes side-panel turns through authenticated node-originated agent requests, threads hosting-node IDs into agent/browser tools, updates macOS/config/schema/plugin SDK surfaces, and adds bridge/browser tests.

PR surface: Source +3999, Tests +539, Config +32, Other +90. Total +4660 across 55 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source inspection of PR head shows the invalid macOS profile write, removed shipped migration, dropped prompt field, public SDK event export, and blocked dependency graph. I did not run the live browser flow because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer review is needed because the remaining blockers are prerequisite merge/rebase, product/security API acceptance, dependency approval, and contributor proof rather than one narrow automated repair.

target.title: feat(browser): My Browser node-anchored turn routing (stacks on #93411)
target.body:
> **Stacked on #93411 â€” that must merge first.** Builds on the run-scoped `gateway.tools.byNode` primitive from #93411; the diff currently overlaps #93411's commits and will be rebased to the extension-only delta once #93411 lands. Draft until then.

## Summary

Makes "My Browser" a usable side-panel copilot on top of node-anchored tool-scoping. Four blocks:

**1. Node-anchored turn routing (the original core).** Routes the side-panel turn **through the hosting node** as an authenticated node-originated `agent.request`, so the gateway confines that turn's tools via `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411), and pins the browser proxy back to that same node. Falls back to a direct gateway turn when no node hosts the bridge.
- Node-host event emitter (`node-event-emitter.ts`, re-exported via `plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime`); extension bridge forwards the side-panel `agent.request` via an `onAgentRequest` callback threaded from `server-lifecycle`; browser tool pins to the turn's `hostingNodeId`.

**2. Browser-copilot persona as a system prompt.** `server-lifecycle` injects `BROWSER_COPILOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` as `extraSystemPrompt` on every node-originated side-panel `agent.request` (server-side, not client-injectable). Without it the agent tends to refuse browser tasks / fetch over HTTP; with it, it acts through the browser tool, defers only CAPTCHA/2FA/payment, and won't reload a page it's already on.

**3. Side-panel UX (`sidepanel.js`/`background.js`).**
- **Per-tab agent sessions** â€” deterministic key per pinned tab (`â€¦:thread:tab-<id>`), so each attached tab has its own resumable conversation; "+ New session" resets the thread.
- **Live tab-context injection** â€” each turn carries the pinned tab's current URL+title, so the agent knows where it is without a tool round-trip and won't re-navigate a page it's on.
- **Streaming render fixes** â€” render the gateway's authoritative full text (no duplicate/garbled deltas), render line breaks, and split inter-tool commentary into separate persisted bubbles with a `â†’ <tool>` step line per tool call (the panel opts into `tool-events`).

**4. Bridge reliability.**
- **Page-only target announce** â€” only real `page` targets are announced to Playwright; worker/`blob:`/service-worker/cross-origin-iframe child targets are dropped (they were announced as phantom pages, hanging the next snapshot ~47s on heavy pages like signup flows).
- **Per-turn CDP keepalive** â€” reuse the cached CDP connection within an idle TTL, force a fresh reconnect after a gap; skip the per-op throwaway health-probe when a live connection is cached.
- **Idempotent `attachTab` + `getTargetId`** â€” re-attaching an already-connected tab no longer crashes Playwright with a Duplicate-target error; the panel can resolve a tab's CDP targetId to pin turns to it.

Out of scope: the `gateway.tools.byNode` primitive itself (#93411). The `driver:"extension"` mode may additionally need the upstream deprecation RFC resolved.

## Linked context

Depends on #93411 (gateway-core `byNode`). Related #93347 (closed â€” superseded by the authenticated-node approach).

## Review findings addressed (local ClawSweeper / Codex review, head 11b72146a8)

A local ClawSweeper review (Codex gpt-5.5, high reasoning) of the prior head flagged six items; all are resolved or explained (fixed in `3335ecebd9`):

1. **[HIGH] Unauthenticated extension bridge** (`extension-bridge-server.ts`) â€” **fixed.** When `gateway.auth.token` is configured, the `/extension` relay handshake must present a matching HMAC on `?token=` (recomputed server-side via WebCrypto, mirroring the extension's `deriveRelayToken`) or the socket is rejected (close 1008); tokenless loopback stays trusted-local. Proven live on the deployed build â€” `no-token â†’ 1008`, `wrong-token â†’ 1008`, `correct-HMAC â†’ accepted` â€” plus 4 unit tests (`extension-bridge-server.auth.test.ts`).
2. **[MED] Hard-coded `DEFAULT_TOKEN`** (`sidepanel.js`) â€” **fixed.** Removed `test-token-for-container`; tokenless loopback now omits auth instead of sending a placeholder credential.
3. **[MED] Broad node-event-emitter SDK surface** (`plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime.ts`) â€” **fixed.** The emitter-install primitives (`registerNodeGatewayEventEmitter`/`hasNodeGatewayEventEmitter`) are no longer re-exported from the plugin-SDK barrel (host-internal, no SDK consumers); only `emitNodeGatewayEvent` remains, for the first-party bridge.
4. **[P1] macOS toggle writes invalid config** (`ConfigStore.swift`) â€” **fixed.** Added `browser.priorDefaultProfile` to the config schema so the My Browser toggle's state write validates against the strict `browser` object.
5. **[P1] `driver:"extension"` collides with the legacy migration** â€” **real; open design decision.** `main` treats `driver:"extension"` as a legacy value the doctor migrates to `existing-session` (Chrome MCP attach; see `doctor-legacy-config.test.ts`), and this branch reuses the same literal for the new node-bridge mode without removing that migration â€” so the doctor would silently rewrite a new-mode `extension` profile back to `existing-session`. Resolution needs either a distinct driver literal for the new mode (keeping the legacy migration + upgrade tests) or coordinating the `extension` deprecation upstream â€” the `driver:"extension"` RFC this PR already flags. Tracked for the rebase / RFC, not a blind code change.
6. **[P1] Dependency-graph churn** (`pnpm-lock.yaml`) â€” **stacked-base drift.** The lockfile diff is upstream override bumps (`@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `hono`, `aws`, `acp`) carried by the #93411-stacked base, not this PR's work; it disappears on the rebase to the extension-only delta noted above.
7. **[P2] `config.example.json` relayPort** â€” **fixed** (`debaa45e3e`). Was `18790`; corrected to `18792` (`DEFAULT_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_PORT`) so the bundled example dials the right bridge port.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** a usable node-anchored browser copilot â€” node-confined turns, a persona that actually drives the browser, per-tab sessions, and a bridge that survives heavy real-world pages.
- **Real environment tested:** an OpenClaw gateway built from this branch driving the bundled extension in a headless Chromium (Xvfb + chromium + ffmpeg) container, plus a device-paired loopback node for the byNode path, plus the standalone bridge + browser-tool unit suites.
- **Evidence after fix (live):**
  - `pnpm build` clean (`BUILD_EXIT=0`).
  - **byNode narrowing** a real node turn: `[byNode] allow=[web_search,image] before=5{tts,web_search,web_fetch,image,canvas} after=2{web_search,image}` (run-scoped via threaded `hostingNodeId`).
  - **Heavy-page (page-only filter):** navigating to `account.proton.me/mail/signup` â€” before, the snapshot timed out at ~47s on ~14 phantom targets; after, `skipped-non-page=2, timeouts=0` and the agent read the live form ("one field: Password").
  - **Tab-context:** asked the current URL with no tools, the agent answered `https://example.org/ â€” "Example Domain"` from injected context with `0` navigations (no reload).
  - **Per-tab isolation:** tab A told a codeword â†’ tab B asked for it â†’ "I don't have a codeword from you in this conversation."
  - **CDP keepalive:** per-op connect churn dropped 7â†’2 across a multi-step turn.
  - **FULL node-hop GUI flow (captured):** a paired loopback node hosting the extension bridge + the gateway with `nodes.browser.node`/`byNode`. A side-panel turn drove Chrome to IANA entirely through the node: gateway log shows the turn attributed to the node (`agent â€¦ node=ffc1b7d3â€¦`) and the browser tool proxied to it (`node.invoke âś“` Ă—3 â†’ `browser.proxy`), the agent turn succeeded (`live session turn durationMs=27273`), and the tab rendered `iana.org` ("Internet Assigned Numbers Authority"). Screenshot: the panel header reads "Connected â€” Internet Assigned Nâ€¦" with the IANA page driven via the node.
  - **Relay auth gate (F1) â€” proven live on the deployed `3335ecebd9` build:** with `gateway.auth.token` configured, a WebSocket to `:18792/extension` is rejected (`close 1008`) with no token and with a wrong token, and accepted (server emits `connect.challenge`) only with the correct `deriveRelayToken` HMAC. Backed by 4 unit tests.
  - **Bootstrap-deadlock fix â€” re-proven on the deployed `3335ecebd9` build:** after killing the node-host (`:18792` â†’ unbound) and restarting it with **no manual relay kick**, the node binds `:18792` at startup and the bridge answers `/json/version`.
- **Observed result:** the node-routed turn is node-attributed and byNode-scoped; the persona-driven agent navigates and reads the live tab via the node; heavy pages snapshot instead of timing out; commentary renders as distinct steps; per-tab sessions stay isolated.
- **Bug found + fixed (node bootstrap deadlock):** the node-host **never eagerly started** the extension bridge â€” it bound `:18792` only lazily inside a `browser.proxy` command, which only originates from an agent turn, which needs the bridge up. Circular â†’ node-anchored browser never worked out of the box. Confirmed: container LISTEN ports were `5900,7799,18789` until a `browser.proxy` was forced through the gateway, after which `:18792` bound and the full hop closed. **Fixed in this PR:** an optional `onNodeHostStart` hook on node-host commands; `runNodeHost` runs registered hooks once after the nodeâ†’gateway emitter is wired; the browser plugin registers a hook that eager-starts the control service (no-op unless a profile uses `driver:"extension"`), via a lazy import so core never statically imports the extension. **Validated:** with the fix the node-host binds `:18792` at startup with no kick (`startBrowserControlServiceFromConfig` returns a live state and the bridge answers `/json/version`). `pnpm build` clean. (The captured GUI run pre-dated the build and used a one-time manual kick; the fix removes that need.)
- **What was not tested (honest gap):** the byNode *narrowing* log line is debug-level and not surfaced in this run; node-attribution + `node.invoke` proxying prove the hop, and `byNode` is configured + active. No remaining flow is unproven.
- **Proof limitations / environment constraints:** gateway + node ran as two processes on one host (loopback pairing) in the `oc-nodeanchor` container (Xvfb + chromium + ffmpeg) â€” the earlier "no VM / disk-blocked" claim is retracted.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm build` clean.
- `extension-bridge-server.agent-request.test.ts` â€” forwards `agent.request` â†’ `onAgentRequest`, error when no routing, empty-message reject, emitter-failure ack (4/4).
- `browser-tool.test.ts` â€” pins to the hosting node, explicit `node=` overrides (browser suite passing, no regressions).
- Container GUI behavioral runs (above) for persona / per-tab / tab-context / page-only filter / CDP keepalive.

## Risk checklist

- **User-visible behavior change?** Yes â€” side panel routes through the node when present (direct-gateway fallback); per-tab sessions; injected tab context; new streaming render + tool-step lines.
- **Config/environment/migration change?** Uses #93411's `gateway.tools.byNode`; no new config.
- **Security/auth/tool-execution change?** Yes â€” turns become node-attributed so byNode can confine them; browser proxy pinned to the hosting node; persona is injected server-side (not client-supplied). Restriction-only via #93411.
- **Highest-risk areas + mitigations:**
  - *Node origination + reply delivery* â†’ bridge only acks; replies use the existing gateway subscription; direct-turn fallback when no node.
  - *Page-only target filter* â†’ only `page` targets announced; same-origin iframes still reached via the page's frame tree, so nothing is lost.
  - *CDP keepalive* â†’ idle-TTL reconnect + the op self-heals if a cached socket is dead.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** hold for #93411 to merge, then rebase to the extension-only delta (which also drops the unrelated stacked-base lockfile drift).
- **Waiting on:** #93411 merge.
- **Addressed:** node-routing + browser-pin + persona + side-panel UX + bridge reliability wired, built clean; bootstrap-deadlock fixed; all six local-review findings resolved or explained (relay-auth gate + 4 unit tests, token hygiene, narrowed SDK surface, config schema; two non-issues explained); combined node-hop flow captured, and the deadlock-fix + relay-auth proven live on the latest build (`3335ecebd9`).
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
